reasoning: The question asks if the WHO will declare a new coronavirus pandemic by December 31, 2025. The initial spark for this market was a February 2025 article about a novel bat coronavirus, which the CDC promptly confirmed was not a public health concern. Searches of WHO statements and global news up to the knowledge cutoff of November 9, 2025, reveal absolutely no reports of a new coronavirus outbreak, let alone one requiring a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) or pandemic declaration. For a massive global pandemic declaration to occur before the end of the year, there would need to be an active, rapidly spreading outbreak already dominating headlines today. Since there is zero evidence of any such outbreak, the likelihood of a resolution to "Yes" in the remaining 7 weeks of the year is virtually nonexistent. The late-October market price of 3% reflects this, and the passage of another 10 days pushes it to the absolute minimum prediction floor.
